source_intake_record.raw_question_text: If the graph of the linear function \(y=kx+b(k\ne 0)\) is shown as in the figure, what is the solution set of the inequality \(kx+b<0\)?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution}: Given $y=kx+b\ (k \neq 0)$ intersects the $x$-axis at $(4,0)$,\\
$\therefore kx+b<0$ has the solution set: $x>4$,\\
thus, the answer is: $\boxed{x>4}$.
extraction_notes: answer field is a worked solution rather than a short final answer
